I INTRODUCTION 
I.1 Product name  
Product name Dataset name 
OMI_HEALTH_CHL_ATLANTIC_OCEANCOLOUR_eutrophication omi_health_atlantic_oceancolour_eutrophication 
I.2 Indicator evaluation method 
The CMEMS Ocean Colour OMIs are derived from the reprocessed (MY) and regional gridded 
chlorophyll products generated and distributed by the CMEMS OC -TAC. The input dataset to 
produce ARC, ATL, and GLO MY are the spectral remote sensing reflectances (Rrs) produced 
by the Plymouth Marine Laboratory (PML) using an ad -hoc configuration for CMEMS of the 
ESA OC-CCI processor version 5.0 to merge at 1km resolution (rather than at 4km as for OC -
CCI) MERIS, MODIS -AQUA, SeaWiFS, VIIRS and OLCI -S3A data. The regional  MY chlorophyll 
datasets are then computed using algorithms optimized for each region of the European 
waters. 
 
Product: OMI_HEALTH_ATLANTIC_OceanColour_eutrophication 
The North Atlantic CMEMS MY Ocean Colour dataset was used to derive a P90 and P10 daily 
climatologies for the 1998 -2017 period. For a given pixel, P90 and P10 were defined as 
dynamic thresholds such as 90% of the 1998-2017 chlorophyll values for that pixel were below 
the P90 value, and 10% of the chlorophyll values were below the P10 value.  
We then computed time series daily area -averaged chlorophyll concentration, daily P90 
climatology and daily P10 climatology. Pixel -area weighted averages were used to calculate 
the daily values. Finally, to generate the indicator eutrophication and oligotr ophication maps 
for a given year, we compared every valid daily observation over the year with the 
corresponding daily climatology on a pixel -by-pixel basis, to determine if values were above 
the P90 threshold, below the P10 threshold or within the [P10, P 90] range. Values above the 
P90 threshold or below the P10 were flagged as anomalous. The number of anomalous and 
total valid observations were stored during this process.  
We then calculated the percentage of valid anomalous observations (above/below the 
P90/P10 thresholds) for each pixel, to create percentile anomaly maps in terms of % days per 
year.  
Finally, we derived an annual indicator map for eutrophication levels: if 25% of the valid 
observations for a given pixel and year were above the P90 thresh old, the pixel was flagged as

eutrophic. Similarly, if 25% of the observations for a given pixel were below the P10 threshold, 
the pixel was flagged as oligotrophic.
